docs.title: 2. Update User Profile
docs.text:
PUT /api/v1/profile

Update profile information for the authenticated user.

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description firstName string No User's first name lastName string No User's last name mobile string No User's mobile phone number faxNumber string No User's fax number streetAddress string No User's street address city string No User's city postCode string No User's postal code gender string No User's gender

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes

docs.title: Example Request

curl -X 'PUT' \

    "firstName": "Mehedi",
    "lastName": "Hassan",
    "mobile": "01812353930",
    "faxNumber": "454",
    "streetAddress": "Dhaka",
    "city": "Dhaka",

docs.title: 3. Upload Profile Image
docs.text:
POST /api/v1/profile/image-upload

Upload a profile image for the authenticated user. The image will be processed and stored with a unique filename.

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description image file Yes Image file (multipart/form-data)

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes Content-Type multipart/form-data Yes

docs.title: Supported Image Formats
docs.text:
• JPEG (.jpg, .jpeg)
  • PNG (.png)
  • GIF (.gif)

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'POST' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/profile/image-upload' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...' \
  -H 'Content-Type: multipart/form-data' \
  -F 'image=@profile.jpg;type=image/jpeg'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text: "73b21d27-466e-45c6-bc2b-0480eb4db2d2.jpg"

docs.title: Response Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description data string Unique filename of the uploaded image

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: This API follows industry-standard error handling patterns (Stripe, GitHub, OpenAI):

docs.title: Success Responses
docs.text:
• HTTP status code indicates success (200, 201, 204)
  • Response body contains data directly (no wrapper object)
  • No isSuccess or statusCode fields needed

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text:
Standard Error (400/401/403/404/500):

{
  "message": "Error description"
}

Validation Error (400):

{
  "message": "Validation failed",
  "errors": [
    {"field": "email", "message": "Invalid email format"},
    {"field": "password", "message": "Password must be at least 8 characters"}
  ]
}

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text:
Status Code Description Example Message 400 Bad Request "Invalid email or password" 401 Unauthorized "Authentication required" 403 Forbidden "Insufficient permissions" 404 Not Found "Product not found" 409 Conflict "User already exists with this email" 500 Internal Server Error "Internal server error"

All error messages are centralized and consistent across all endpoints.

docs.title: Refund Request API
docs.text:
This documentation provides comprehensive details for the Refund Request API endpoints.

Base URL: http://localhost:8080

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: All Refund Request endpoints require Bearer token authentication.

docs.title: Refund Request Endpoints
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description Authentication Required POST /api/v1/refund-requests/{orderId} Create a refund request Yes (Customer) GET /api/v1/refund-requests/{id} Get refund request details Yes GET /api/v1/seller/refund-requests/order/{orderId} Get refunds for an order (Seller) Yes (Seller) PUT /api/v1/seller/refund-requests/{id}/status Update refund status (Seller) Yes (Seller) POST /api/v1/refund-requests/{id}/ship Mark refund as shipped Yes (Customer) GET /api/v1/admin/refund-requests/order/{orderId} Get refunds for an order (Admin) Yes (Admin) PUT /api/v1/admin/refund-requests/{id}/status Update refund status (Admin) Yes (Admin)

docs.title: 1. Update Refund Status (Seller)
docs.text:
PUT /api/v1/seller/refund-requests/{id}/status

Update the status of a refund request (APPROVED, REJECTED, etc.).

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'PUT' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/seller/refund-requests/refund-uuid-123/status' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <seller_token>' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
  "status": "APPROVED",
  "refundAmount": 50.0,
  "refundMethod": "ORIGINAL"
}'

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: Status Code Description 400 Bad Request 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden (User does not own the order/shop) 404 Not Found

docs.title: Review Rating API
docs.text:
This documentation provides comprehensive details for the Review Rating API endpoints. The API supports creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting product reviews and ratings within the platform. Reviews are associated with specific products and users, enabling a complete feedback system for e-commerce platforms.

Base URL: http://localhost:8080

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All Review Rating endpoints require Bearer token authentication. Include the access token in the Authorization header:

Authorization: Bearer <your_access_token>

docs.title: Review Rating Endpoints
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description Authentication Required GET /api/v1/reviews Get all Reviews Yes POST /api/v1/reviews Create a New Review Yes PUT /api/v1/reviews/{id} Update Review Yes DELETE /api/v1/reviews/{id} Delete Review Yes

docs.title: 1. Get Reviews
docs.text:
GET /api/v1/reviews

Retrieve reviews for a specific product with optional pagination support.

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description productId string Yes UUID of the product to get reviews for limit number No Maximum number of reviews to return (default: 20) offset number No Number of reviews to skip for pagination (default: 0)

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'GET' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/reviews?productId=cbd630f6-bf9f-48ad-ac51-f806807d99fd&limit=10' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "70ac842b-7a81-4976-9564-d440880d1736",
      "userId": "a67fd0cc-3d92-4259-bbd4-1e0ba49dece4",
      "productId": "cbd630f6-bf9f-48ad-ac51-f806807d99fd",
      "reviewText": "Good product",
      "rating": 2
    }
  ],
  "metadata": {
    "totalCount": 1,
    "limit": 10,
    "offset": 0
  }
}

docs.title: Response Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description data array Array of review objects data[].id string Unique identifier for the review data[].userId string UUID of the user who created the review data[].productId string UUID of the reviewed product data[].reviewText string Text content of the review data[].rating number Numerical rating (typically 1-5)

docs.title: 2. Create Review
docs.text:
POST /api/v1/reviews

Create a new review and rating for a product. The review will be associated with the authenticated user and specified product.

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Field Type Required Description productId string Yes UUID of the product being reviewed rating number Yes Numerical rating for the product reviewText string Yes Text content of the review

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes Content-Type application/json Yes

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'POST' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/reviews' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
  "productId": "cbd630f6-bf9f-48ad-ac51-f806807d99fd",
  "rating": 2,
  "reviewText": "Good product"
}'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
    "id": "70ac842b-7a81-4976-9564-d440880d1736",
    "userId": "a67fd0cc-3d92-4259-bbd4-1e0ba49dece4",
    "productId": "cbd630f6-bf9f-48ad-ac51-f806807d99fd",
    "reviewText": "Good product",
    "rating": 2
  }
}

docs.title: Response Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description id string Unique identifier for the created review userId string UUID of the user who created the review productId string UUID of the reviewed product reviewText string Text content of the review rating number Numerical rating for the product

docs.title: 3. Update Review
docs.text:
PUT /api/v1/reviews/{id}

Update an existing review by its ID. You can modify both the review text and rating.

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description id string Yes Unique identifier of the review to update

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description review string No Updated review text content rating number No Updated numerical rating

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'PUT' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/reviews/70ac842b-7a81-4976-9564-d440880d1736?review=Product%20review%20edited&rating=5' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
    "id": "70ac842b-7a81-4976-9564-d440880d1736",
    "userId": "a67fd0cc-3d92-4259-bbd4-1e0ba49dece4",
    "productId": "cbd630f6-bf9f-48ad-ac51-f806807d99fd",
    "reviewText": "Product review edited",
    "rating": 5
  }
}

docs.title: Response Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description id string Unique identifier of the updated review userId string UUID of the user who created the review productId string UUID of the reviewed product reviewText string Updated review text content rating number Updated numerical rating

docs.title: 4. Delete Review
docs.text:
DELETE /api/v1/reviews/{id}

Delete a review by its ID. This operation permanently removes the review from the system.

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description id string Yes Unique identifier of the review to delete

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'DELETE' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/reviews/70ac842b-7a81-4976-9564-d440880d1736' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...'

docs.title: Response Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description data string ID of the deleted review

  • Response body contains data directly (no wrapper object)
  • No isSuccess or statusCode fields needed

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text:

Validation Error (400):

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text:

docs.title: Shipping Address API
docs.text:
This documentation provides details for the Shipping Address API endpoints. The API supports managing multiple saved shipping addresses for authenticated users.

Base URL: http://localhost:8080/api/v1/checkout/shipping-address

[!NOTE] This module has been consolidated into the Checkout module. While the previous endpoints are maintained for backward compatibility, it is recommended to use the /checkout/shipping-address prefix for new integrations.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All Shipping Address endpoints require Bearer token authentication. Include the access token in the Authorization header:

Authorization: Bearer <your_access_token>

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description Authentication Required GET / Retrieve all shipping addresses for the authenticated user Yes POST / Add a new shipping address Yes PUT /{id} Update an existing shipping address Yes DELETE /{id} Delete a shipping address Yes

docs.title: 1. Get All Shipping Addresses
docs.text:
GET /

Retrieve all shipping addresses associated with the authenticated user.

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
[
  {
    "id": "5489a8b4-7a16-4854-b157-396a8a731032",
    "userId": "a67fd0cc-3d92-4259-bbd4-1e0ba49dece4",
    "firstName": "John",
    "lastName": "Doe",
    "email": "john.doe@example.com",
    "phoneNumber": "+1234567890",
    "streetAddress": "123 Main St",
    "city": "New York",
    "state": "NY",
    "country": "USA",
    "zipCode": "10001",
    "isDefault": true
  }
]

docs.title: 2. Add Shipping Address
docs.text:
POST /

Add a new shipping address for the authenticated user.

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Field Type Required Description firstName string Yes First name of the recipient lastName string Yes Last name of the recipient email string Yes Email for shipping contact phoneNumber string Yes Phone number for shipping contact streetAddress string Yes Street address city string Yes City state string No State or province country string Yes Country zipCode string Yes Zip or postal code isDefault boolean No Whether this is the default address (default: false)

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
{
  "firstName": "John",
  "lastName": "Doe",
  "email": "john.doe@example.com",
  "phoneNumber": "+1234567890",
  "streetAddress": "123 Main St",
  "city": "New York",
  "state": "NY",
  "country": "USA",
  "zipCode": "10001",
  "isDefault": true
}

docs.title: 3. Update Shipping Address
docs.text:
PUT /{id}

Update an existing shipping address.

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description id string Yes Unique identifier of the shipping address to update

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Same as Add Shipping Address.

docs.title: 4. Delete Shipping Address
docs.text:
DELETE /{id}

Delete a shipping address by its ID.

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description id string Yes Unique identifier of the shipping address to delete

docs.title: Shipping Method API
docs.text:
This documentation provides details for the Shipping Method API endpoints. Shipping methods define delivery options, prices, and estimated delivery times.

Base URL: http://localhost:8080/api/v1/checkout/shipping-method (Public) Admin URL: http://localhost:8080/api/v1/admin/shipping-methods (Admin)

[!NOTE] Public lookup for shipping methods has been consolidated into the Checkout module at /checkout/shipping-method. Management operations (Create/Update/Delete) are now consistently located under the /admin/shipping-methods prefix.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
• GET requests are public and do not require authentication.
  • POST, PUT, and DELETE requests require Admin authentication.
Authorization: Bearer <admin_access_token>

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description Authentication Required Role GET / Retrieve all available shipping methods No Any POST / Create a new shipping method Yes Admin PUT /{id} Update an existing shipping method Yes Admin DELETE /{id} Delete a shipping method Yes Admin

docs.title: 1. Get All Shipping Methods
docs.text:
GET /

Retrieve all available shipping methods.

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
[
  {
    "id": "1",
    "name": "Standard Shipping",
    "type": "Economy",
    "price": 5.0,
    "deliveryTime": "3-5 business days"
  },
  {
    "id": "2",
    "name": "Express Shipping",
    "type": "Premium",
    "price": 15.0,
    "deliveryTime": "1-2 business days"
  }
]

docs.title: 2. Create Shipping Method (Admin)
docs.text:
POST /

Create a new shipping method.

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Field Type Required Description name string Yes Name of the shipping method type string No Type of shipping (e.g., Economy, Premium) price double Yes Cost of shipping deliveryTime string No Estimated delivery time

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
{
  "name": "Overnight Shipping",
  "type": "Urgent",
  "price": 25.0,
  "deliveryTime": "Next business day"
}

docs.title: 3. Update Shipping Method (Admin)
docs.text:
PUT /{id}

Update an existing shipping method.

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description id string Yes Unique identifier of the shipping method to update

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Same as Create Shipping Method.

docs.title: 4. Delete Shipping Method (Admin)
docs.text:
DELETE /{id}

Delete a shipping method by its ID.

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description id string Yes Unique identifier of the shipping method to delete

docs.title: Shop Category API
docs.text:
This documentation provides comprehensive details for the Shop Category API endpoints. The API supports creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting shop categories. These endpoints are typically used by administrators to manage the categorization system for shops within the platform.

Base URL: http://localhost:8080

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All Shop Category endpoints require Bearer token authentication with admin privileges. Include the access token in the Authorization header:

Authorization: Bearer <your_access_token>

docs.title: Shop Category Endpoints
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description Authentication Required POST /api/v1/admin/shop-categories Create a new shop category Yes (Admin) GET /api/v1/admin/shop-categories Retrieve list of shop categories Yes (Admin) PUT /api/v1/admin/shop-categories/{id} Update an existing shop category Yes (Admin) DELETE /api/v1/admin/shop-categories/{id} Delete a shop category Yes (Admin)

docs.title: 1. Create Shop Category
docs.text:
POST /api/v1/admin/shop-categories

Create a new shop category. This endpoint allows administrators to add new categories for organizing shops.

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description name string Yes Name of the shop category

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes Content-Type application/json Yes

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'POST' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/admin/shop-categories' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "name": "New digital Shop"
  }'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
    "id": "28918963-f932-425b-884b-a34d8ae69b2a",
    "name": "New digital Shop"
  }
}

docs.title: Response Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description id string Unique identifier for the created shop category name string Name of the shop category

docs.title: 2. Get Shop Categories
docs.text:
GET /api/v1/admin/shop-categories

Retrieve a list of shop categories with optional pagination support.

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description limit number No Maximum number of categories to return (default: 20) offset number No Number of categories to skip for pagination (default: 0)

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'GET' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/admin/shop-categories?limit=10' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "58f5c085-d04a-47de-beab-1d476b6ce432",
      "name": "Electronics"
    },
    {
      "id": "75b44e08-2c94-438f-b500-b204c7c90cca",
      "name": "Fashion"
    }
  ],
  "metadata": {
    "totalCount": 2,
    "limit": 10,
    "offset": 0
  }
}

docs.title: Response Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description data array Array of shop category objects data[].id string Unique identifier for the shop category data[].name string Name of the shop category metadata object Pagination metadata metadata.totalCount number Total number of items metadata.limit number Limit applied metadata.skip number Offset applied

docs.title: 3. Update Shop Category
docs.text:
PUT /api/v1/admin/shop-categories/{id}

Update an existing shop category by its ID.

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description id string Yes Unique identifier of the shop category to update

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description name string Yes New name for the shop category

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'PUT' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/admin/shop-categories/9c95c44c-3767-4ca2-9486-e28e390b3741?name=Piash%20Digital%20shop' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
    "id": "9c95c44c-3767-4ca2-9486-e28e390b3741",
    "name": "Piash Digital shop"
  }
}

docs.title: Response Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description id string Unique identifier of the updated shop category name string Updated name of the shop category

docs.title: 4. Delete Shop Category
docs.text:
DELETE /api/v1/admin/shop-categories/{id}

Delete a shop category by its ID. This operation permanently removes the category from the system.

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description id string Yes Unique identifier of the shop category to delete

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes

docs.title: Example Request

  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/admin/shop-categories/9c95c44c-3767-4ca2-9486-e28e390b3741' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text: "2a17da31-7517-41db-b7d3-f77d0ddd52a5"

docs.title: Response Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description data string ID of the deleted shop category

docs.title: Shop API
docs.text:
This documentation provides comprehensive details for the Shop API endpoints. The API supports creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting shops within the platform. Shops are associated with specific categories and can be managed by administrators or authorized users.

Base URL: http://localhost:8080

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Most Shop endpoints require Bearer token authentication. Include the access token in the Authorization header:

Authorization: Bearer <your_access_token>

docs.title: Shop Endpoints (V1)
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description Authentication Required POST /api/v1/seller/shops Create a new shop Yes (Seller) GET /api/v1/seller/shops Retrieve list of owned shops Yes (Seller) PUT /api/v1/seller/shops/{id} Update an existing shop Yes (Seller) GET /api/v1/shops/{id} Retrieve detailed info about a specific shop No GET /api/v1/shops/public Retrieve list of public shops with filters No GET /api/v1/shops/category/{categoryId} Retrieve shops by category No GET /api/v1/shops/featured Retrieve featured shops No GET /api/v1/admin/shops/status Retrieve shops by status Yes (Admin) PUT /api/v1/admin/shops/approve/{id} Approve a shop Yes (Admin) PUT /api/v1/admin/shops/reject/{id} Reject a shop Yes (Admin) PUT /api/v1/admin/shops/suspend/{id} Suspend a shop Yes (Admin) PUT /api/v1/admin/shops/activate/{id} Activate a shop Yes (Admin)

docs.title: Shop Endpoints (V2)
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description Authentication Required PUT /api/v2/seller/shops/{shopId} Optimized shop update with source tracking Yes (Seller)

docs.title: 1. Create Shop (V1)
docs.text:
POST /api/v1/seller/shops

Create a new shop with a specified name and category. The shop will be associated with the authenticated seller.

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes Content-Type application/json Yes

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Field Type Required Description name string Yes Name of the shop categoryId string Yes UUID of the shop category

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'POST' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/seller/shops' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "name": "Royal Shop",
    "categoryId": "5e67ec97-9ed6-48ee-9d56-4163fe1711cb"
  }'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
    "id": "cbfdcfa3-fb65-4fa3-9078-e0f8cc63ddbc",
    "name": "Royal Shop"
}

docs.title: 2. Update Shop (V2 Optimized)
docs.text:
PUT /api/v2/seller/shops/{shopId}

Update an existing shop with enhanced metadata and a cleaner response structure.

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description shopId string Yes Unique identifier of the shop to update

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description source string No Source of the update (e.g., 'mobile', 'web')

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Field Type Required Description name string No New name for the shop categoryId string No New category ID

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'PUT' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v2/seller/shops/a33b8912-e0b2-4058-9d7b-3c7ef9b935c7?source=mobile_app' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "name": "Updated Shop V2"
  }'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
    "v2_data": {
        "id": "a33b8912-e0b2-4058-9d7b-3c7ef9b935c7",
        "name": "Updated Shop V2",
        "categoryId": "9c95c44c-3767-4ca2-9486-e28e390b3741"
    },
    "source": "mobile_app"
}

docs.title: 3. Get Public Shops (V1)
docs.text:
GET /api/v1/shops/public

Retrieve a list of public shops with optional status and category filters.

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description status string No Shop status to filter by (APPROVED, etc.) category string No UUID of the category to filter by limit number No Maximum number of shops to return offset number No Number of shops to skip

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'GET' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/shops/public?status=APPROVED&limit=10' \
  -H 'accept: application/json'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "a33b8912-e0b2-4058-9d7b-3c7ef9b935c7",
      "name": "Shop Name",
      "status": "APPROVED"
    }
  ],
  "metadata": {
    "totalCount": 1,
    "limit": 10,
    "offset": 0
  }
}

docs.title: 4. Admin Shop Approval
docs.text:
PUT /api/v1/admin/shops/approve/{id}

Approve a pending shop application (Admin only).

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'PUT' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/admin/shops/approve/a33b8912-e0b2-4058-9d7b-3c7ef9b935c7' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <admin_token>'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
    "id": "a33b8912-e0b2-4058-9d7b-3c7ef9b935c7",
    "status": "APPROVED"
}

docs.title: Success Responses
docs.text: • HTTP 200/201/204: Operation successful. Data returned directly.

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text: Status Code Description 400 Bad Request (Validation failed) 401 Unauthorized (Invalid token) 403 Forbidden (Insufficient roles) 404 Not Found (Resource does not exist) 410 Gone (API version deprecated) 500 Internal Server Error

docs.title: Wishlist API
docs.text:
This documentation provides comprehensive details for the Wishlist API endpoints. The API supports adding products to wishlist, retrieving user's wishlist items, checking product status, and removing products from wishlist. Wishlist functionality is user-specific and requires authentication to access personal wishlist data.

Base URL: http://localhost:8080

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All Wishlist endpoints require Bearer token authentication. Include the access token in the Authorization header:

Authorization: Bearer <your_access_token>

docs.title: Wishlist Endpoints
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description Authentication Required POST /api/v1/wishlists Add a product to wishlist Yes GET /api/v1/wishlists Retrieve user's wishlist items (Paginated) Yes GET /api/v1/wishlists/check Check if a product is in wishlist Yes DELETE /api/v1/wishlists/remove Remove a product from wishlist Yes

docs.title: 1. Add Product to Wishlist
docs.text:
POST /api/v1/wishlists

Add a product to the authenticated user's wishlist.

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description productId string Yes UUID of the product to add to wishlist

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes Content-Type application/json Yes

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'POST' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/wishlists' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
  "productId": "5b24d429-c981-47c8-9318-f4d61dd2c1a4"
}'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
    "product": {
      "id": "5b24d429-c981-47c8-9318-f4d61dd2c1a4",
      "categoryId": "58f5c085-d04a-47de-beab-1d476b6ce432",
      "name": "Polo T Shirt",
      // ... product details
    }
  }
}

docs.title: 2. Get Wishlist Items
docs.text:
GET /api/v1/wishlists

Retrieve all products in the authenticated user's wishlist with pagination.

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description limit number No Maximum number of items to return (default: 20) offset number No Number of items to skip for pagination (default: 0)

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'GET' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/wishlists?page=1&limit=10' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "5b24d429-c981-47c8-9318-f4d61dd2c1a4",
      "name": "Polo T Shirt"
      // ... product details
    }
  ],
  "metadata": {
    "totalCount": 1,
    "limit": 10,
    "offset": 0
  }
}

docs.title: 3. Check Product in Wishlist
docs.text:
GET /api/v1/wishlists/check

Check if a specific product exists in the user's wishlist.

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description productId string Yes UUID of the product to check

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'GET' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/wishlists/check?productId=5b24d429-c981-47c8-9318-f4d61dd2c1a4' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text: true

docs.title: 4. Remove Product from Wishlist
docs.text:
DELETE /api/v1/wishlists/remove

Remove a specific product from the authenticated user's wishlist.

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description productId string Yes UUID of the product to remove from wishlist

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Value Required Authorization Bearer <access_token> Yes

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X 'DELETE' \
  'http://localhost:8080/api/v1/wishlists/remove?productId=5b24d429-c981-47c8-9318-f4d61dd2c1a4' \
  -H 'accept: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...'

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
      "id": "5b24d429-c981-47c8-9318-f4d61dd2c1a4",
      "name": "Polo T Shirt",
      // ... product details
  }
}

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: This API follows industry-standard error handling patterns (Stripe, GitHub, OpenAI):

docs.title: Success Responses
docs.text:
• HTTP status code indicates success (200, 201, 204)
  • Response body contains data directly (no wrapper object)
  • No isSuccess or statusCode fields needed

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text:
Standard Error (400/401/403/404/500):

{
  "message": "Error description"
}

Validation Error (400):

{
  "message": "Validation failed",
  "errors": [
    {"field": "email", "message": "Invalid email format"},
    {"field": "password", "message": "Password must be at least 8 characters"}
  ]
}

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text:
Status Code Description Example Message 400 Bad Request "Invalid email or password" 401 Unauthorized "Authentication required" 403 Forbidden "Insufficient permissions" 404 Not Found "Product not found" 409 Conflict "User already exists with this email" 500 Internal Server Error "Internal server error"

All error messages are centralized and consistent across all endpoints.
